summary: Iter75 — full E2E verification of PROJEXINO playbook redesign (5 new themes + alias mapping + clickable PDF URLs + full_page image sections + Make-it-as-PDF flow), email-template mobile scale-to-fit preview, and testimonials-tile removal from launchpad. Backend pytest (7/7 pass) + frontend playwright (all flows pass). Zero console errors, no data left behind.
critical_code_review_comments: playbooks.py L38 _resolve_theme falls back to future_tech on unknown key silently — good runtime guard, but the DB retains the legacy theme string (e.g. 'ivory') indefinitely because create/update never normalise. Consider persisting the resolved key on write so future analytics/edits see the canonical value. Today the frontend BookCover also resolves via ALIASES so there is no user-facing bug, just data hygiene.

playbooks.py _linkify uses a single re.sub for URLs but does NOT escape & inside URLs before wrapping in ReportLab <a href='..'> — if a URL contains &param=1 the substituted string will render as &param=1 in one place and unescaped raw & in another after the surrounding replace('&','&') pipeline. Add a targeted escape inside _linkify or run _linkify AFTER html-escape (which is the current order) and escape the URL body separately. Not blocking today (test URLs pass), but any query-string URL will double-escape.

Playbooks.jsx pb-pages-input handler passes ALL images through the file reader without size validation — dropping a 20MB PNG will still base64-embed the entire payload into the JSON body and blow up PDF size / MongoDB doc size. Add a client-side size guard (e.g. reject > 3MB, or auto-downscale via canvas) before storing image_b64.

playbooks.py _build_playbook_pdf full_page section catches 'except Exception: pass' silently — a malformed image_b64 (wrong padding, corrupted decode) will produce an invisible missing page with zero user feedback. Log the failure so we can surface it in the shelf UI (e.g. 'This playbook has 1 broken page image').

Playbooks.jsx BookCover uses whileHover 3D rotate but no reduced-motion query — users with prefers-reduced-motion:reduce will still see the tilt. Wrap the whileHover with useReducedMotion() from framer-motion to respect the OS setting.

EmailTemplates.jsx PreviewModal.scale calc uses cw from a resize listener but NOT from ResizeObserver on the modal itself — if the parent modal resizes (e.g. content grows) without a window resize, the scale won't update. Not visible today but worth a ResizeObserver for robustness.
seed_data_creation: Created + immediately deleted 2 test playbooks (TEST_iter75_future_tech, TEST_iter75_midnight_alias) via backend API. Created + deleted 1 test playbook (TEST_E2E Pages Test iter75 → slug test-e2e-pages-test-iter75) via UI. Zero test data remains. Real client-onboarding-playbook is untouched and still on shelf rendering with new Minimal Clean design (ivory→minimal_clean alias mapping working).
context_for_next_testing_agent: GET /api/playbooks/themes returns exactly {future_tech, minimal_clean, creative_edge, corporate_pro, nature}. Legacy theme keys (midnight, ivory, noir, royal, emerald) are accepted on POST/PATCH via _valid_theme, but the DB keeps the raw value — the frontend BookCover + backend PDF cover both resolve at render time via THEME_ALIASES. Existing playbook 'client-onboarding-playbook' is stored with theme='ivory' and displays as 'Minimal Clean'. Verified /URI link annotations present in PDF for both future_tech (2 URIs from URL body + 3 from bottom bar clickable links) and midnight-alias (bottom-bar clickable links). BookCover contains 3D whileHover tilt (transformStyle:preserve-3d, perspective:900) — no crashes on hover. Test-ids: pb-new-btn, pb-theme-{future_tech|minimal_clean|creative_edge|corporate_pro|nature}, pb-add-pages, pb-pages-input (hidden file input, use set_input_files directly), pb-add-section, pb-smart-paste-toggle, pb-smart-paste-text, pb-smart-paste-append, pb-smart-paste-replace, pb-sec-add-table-{i}, pb-sec-{i}-cell-{ri}-{ci}, pb-save (Save changes / Create playbook — no PDF), pb-make-pdf (save + PDF preview), pb-open-{slug}, pb-delete-{slug}, pb-live-preview, pdf-canvas-viewer, pdf-preview-modal. Email preview: /app/email-templates redirects to /app/outreach?tab=templates — templates tab pane has data-testid=out-tab-templates-pane. tpl-preview-{slug} on each card; tpl-preview-modal / tpl-preview-canvas / tpl-preview-w-{phone|tablet|desktop} / tpl-preview-close / tpl-preview-send-test. Mobile 375px: modal is 343 wide, canvas is 351 wide (transform:scale — always <= viewport). Switching to desktop or tablet mode still keeps canvas <= 375 because the scale=min(1, (cw-24)/widthPx). Desktop 1280px: canvas renders at natural 720px. Launchpad: launch-card-testimonials no longer in CARDS_PORTAL — verified DOM absent; launch-card-clients / -contracts / -digi still present.
rca_of_the_issue: No bugs to root-cause. Backend: 7/7 pytest cases pass — themes endpoint returns exactly the 5 new keys with labels/accents; POST accepts both future_tech and legacy midnight (via THEME_ALIASES→_valid_theme); PDF for the future_tech playbook and midnight-alias playbook both contain /URI annotations (clickable links) and the 'projexino.com' string; deletion works; real playbook untouched. Frontend: Mobile 375px email preview modal scales the iframe canvas to fit (measured 351px within 375 viewport, right edge 363), width switcher visible and switching still keeps canvas within viewport; desktop 1280 shows normal 720px canvas. Playbook builder shows 5 theme swatches with correct data-testids; pb-pages-input accepts multiple PNGs and creates FULL PAGE sections (verified: 4 'FULL PAGE' text nodes = 2 in sections list + 2 in live preview); Make it as PDF saves the playbook AND opens the PDF preview modal with 4 rendered canvases within 3s. Shelf entry for the new test playbook appears with the new BookCover design (V1.0·2026 chip, two-tone title, feature strip, bottom bar www.projexino.com + hello@projexino.com); pb-delete-{slug} cleans up. Alias mapping confirmed on the shelf: Client Onboarding Playbook (theme='ivory' in DB) displays 'Minimal Clean' label under the cover. Testimonials tile absent from launchpad; other tiles (Clients/Contracts/Digi/HR/Playbooks/Website Config) present.
